concerns.text: Task T019b mandates verifying gradients flow 'THROUGH the GFM decoder' to the solver. This violates Spec FR-003 and Constitution Principle VI, which explicitly state the GFM is frozen and gradients must NOT flow through the decoder weights. The task describes a semantic weakening of the 'frozen' constraint by implying backpropagation through the frozen backbone.

Task T009 includes a placeholder '[UNRESOLVED-CLAIM: c_9f485eb8 — status=not_enough_info]' within the task description. This violates the constraint that tasks must be actionable and complete; a task cannot be executed if it contains unresolved claims about its own deliverable scope or verification criteria.

Task T021b records 'one-second collision-free duration' but lacks the explicit logic to calculate this duration from raw logs to validate SC-001. The spec defines success as maintaining this duration; the task only mentions recording it. Without a calculation/verification step, the acceptance criterion for SC-001 is silently dropped.

Task T022a mandates running the 'baseline GAM (neural predictor)' on CPU-only hardware. The spec (FR-002/FR-004) and Constitution do not authorize a CPU-optimized variant of the baseline; the baseline is defined as the original GAM. This task introduces a silent simplification (hardware adaptation) not present in the spec, violating the 'no silent constitution drift' principle.
